entry.title: My First “Fan Email” [cheers]
entry.content:
Today I received an email. Seeing the subject line of the mail, I was confused – ’cause I’ve never received a mail like this. The subject line read: 163 - growing your online presence.

Giving a quick glance, I realized that it turned out to be a “fan email” about my site, though not really about my site at all. Well, it’s a sales pitch, and obviously, the sender had NOT actually read what my site is about.

My first fan email ~ haha

Anyway, thank you for your attention; if you are interested or just feel curious, please drop by at anytime. : )

entry.title: About Jonathan Lee
entry.content:
Jonathan Lee (Lee Zongsheng)

Jonathan Lee is a lyrist, composer and singer from Taiwan, China.

He was born and raised in a ordinary family in 1958. His mother was a middle school teacher, and his father was a gas station owner.


Major Experience and Important Works

Childhood and Youth

Entering the Music Industry

Rock Records

Career as a Singer

Recent Years


Stories and Anecdote about Jonathan


Stories and Anecdote about Jonathan


Recommend Works and Corresponding Versions
entry.summary: an extremely talented lyrist and composer, my favorite singer

entry.title: Really - Bei Dao
entry.content:
Really

Bei Dao


The thick fog has whitewashed every tree trunk,

In the horse stable's disheveled mane,

Wild bees dance. The green flood

Is but the dawn held back by the embankment.


This morning,

I forgot our age.

Ice is cracking, pebbles

Have left our fingerprints on the water's surface.


Really, this is spring,

A leaping heart stirs the clouds in the water.

Spring has no nationality,

White clouds are citizens of the world.

Let's make peace with humanity,

My song.


※ Translated by Deepseek



original version (in Chinese):


真的

北岛


浓雾涂白了每一颗树干，

马棚披散的长发中，

野蜂飞舞。

绿色的洪水

只是那被堤岸阻隔的黎明。


在这个早晨，

我忘记了我们的年龄。

冰在龟裂，

石子在水面留下了我们的指纹。


真的，这就是春天呵，

狂跳的心搅乱水中的浮云。

春天是没有国籍的，

白云是世界的公民。

和人类言归于好吧，

我的歌声。

entry.title: Welcome to my website!
entry.content:
Hi, there. Welcome to my website!

I'm a young researcher (well, at least I think I am) from China, studying topology-guided dynamic stimuli-responsive framework material design.

So this is my website, Crease in Frame. Actually, it would be more like a collection of memorable works, and would involve a lot of my personal thoughts and study notes. Hopefully, this site can be of help for you.

As you have seen at the home page, this website will focus on the topic Creases, Connections, and Sensing, which is also my researcher philosophy. This topic can be explained as three parts:

· Topology-guided: unraveling the mathematical and physical mechanisms behind the design and structural change of chemical devices

· Intelligent: (1) focusing on stimuli-responsive frameworks; (2) integrating with computational chemistry; (3) exploring wavelet analysis of the wave function to understand electronic behaviours

· Dynamic: focusing on the unique properties brought by dynamic structural changes, and developing responsive nanoscale devices

Just as a crease alters the morphology and structural locking of a sheet, I am fascinated by how subtle topological changes of frameworks can trigger macroscopic intelligence and truly benefit our life.


Chen Yiru

Framework Materials MOFs, COFs, MOPs etc.

Interested in:

· topology-guided design of dynamic stimuli-responsive framework materials

· responsive devices development at the nanoscale

· assisted with computational chemistry; wavelet analysis of the wave function

Email: 13606751719@163.com
Welcome to exchange ideas!

entry.title: the Structure of the Site
entry.content:
Welcome to Crease in Frame, and here are some explanation about the website structure.

The website will focus on the topic Creases, Connections, and Sensing, involving mutiple desciplines and also including some personal thoughts and feelings of my own life.

The content would include:
· relevant knowledge
· representative reseachers
· memorable works
· study notes and thoughts
· my works
· entertainment topics: harmonica, misic, novels, plogs about my life ect.

※ The content involved in the site are all collected or created by me, so it is inevitably tinged with subjective bias; please don't mind.


Navigation Column

There are several divisions you can reach directly in the navigation column, namely:
· Blogs
· Topology
· Responsive
· Origamic&Dynamic
· Quantum Chem
· For Devices
· Works


Blogs

It is the largest and most-actively-update part of the site and covers various topics, mainly about my thoughts and notes.

There are plenty of tags and rough categories to help for searching. I'll explain for you now.

Categories

Blogs

Notes

Plogs

Writings

Tags

In the Blog session, you will see a poem line, "Spring is the Citizen of the World" witten by a Chinese poet, Bei Dao. It is adapted from my favorate poem, Really. The original version is:

Spring has no nationality,
while white clouds are citizens of the world.
To make peace with humanity,
I'll sing for it.

The tranquility and content depicted within the lines attract me deeply. Hope you can experience such a mood here, too. Well, enjoy your journey here.

Have fun!
entry.summary: the content of the site and how you can get in
